messages.content: convert hinglish अवेयरनेस सेक्शन में बताती हूं आपको।
In English, "अवेयरनेस सेक्शन में बताती हूं आपको।" translates to "I will tell you in the awareness section."
convert hinglish
The Hinglish version of "अवेयरनेस सेक्शन में बताती हूं आपको।" would be "Awareness section mein bataati hoon aapko."

सबसे बड़ा पॉइंट यह है हमारे यहां पर कि
जो चीज स्किल मेरे पास है और जो हथियार मेरे

The Hinglish version of the given Hindi text "सबसे बड़ा पॉइंट यह है हमारे यहां पर कि जो चीज स्किल मेरे पास है और जो हथियार मेरे" could be something like:

"Biggest point yeh hai hamare yahan par ki jo cheez skill mere paas hai aur jo hathiyar mere..."

(Note that the sentence seems incomplete as it doesn't finish after "mere", perhaps you were going to mention "mere paas hai" or something similar to complete the sentence about the skills and tools or weapons the speaker possesses.)
not change convert hinglish

The provided Hindi text seems to be an incomplete thought. To convert the part you've provided into Hinglish, it would read:

"Sabse bada point yeh hai hamare yahan par ki jo cheez skill mere paas hai aur jo hathiyar mere..."

Here, each Hindi word is written using the Latin alphabet, staying true to the original Hindi sentence structure and meaning. However, the sentence appears to cut off before completion, so the full context or intended message may not be fully communicated with this fragment.

पास है स्पॉन्सरशिप लाने के, वह मैं आपके
साथ शेयर नहीं करना चाहती

The complete sentence in Hindi:

"सबसे बड़ा पॉइंट यह है हमारे यहां पर कि जो चीज स्किल मेरे पास है और जो हथियार मेरे पास है स्पॉन्सरशिप लाने के, वह मैं आपके साथ शेयर नहीं करना चाहती।"

The Hinglish translation would be:

"Sabse bada point yeh hai hamare yahan par ki jo cheez skill mere paas hai aur jo hathiyar mere paas hai sponsorship laane ke, woh main aapke saath share nahi karna chahti."

This translation retains the Hindi sentence structure and uses Roman letters to spell out the Hindi words.
पास है स्पॉन्सरशिप लाने के, वह मैं आपके साथ शेयर नहीं करना चाहती

The Hinglish version of "पास है स्पॉन्सरशिप लाने के, वह मैं आपके साथ शेयर नहीं करना चाहती" is:

"Paas hai sponsorship laane ke, woh main aapke saath share nahi karna chahti."

This phrase translates in English to:

"I have the skill and the means to bring in sponsorship, but I do not want to share that with you."
क्योंकि मुझे डर है कि आप मुझसे आगे बढ़ जाएंगे। जब मैंने स्टार्ट किया क्योंकि मुझे

The Hinglish version of "क्योंकि मुझे डर है कि आप मुझसे आगे बढ़ जाएंगे। जब मैंने स्टार्ट किया क्योंकि मुझे" is:

"Kyunki mujhe dar hai ki aap mujhse aage badh jaayenge. Jab maine start kiya kyunki mujhe..."

This appears to be an incomplete sentence where the speaker is expressing a fear that the other person will progress ahead of them, starting with the reason they began something but the sentence does not finish. The continuation is missing for a complete context.
गाइड करने वाले कोई नहीं थे तो मैं झट से धक्के मार कर इधर से, उधर से,

Combining the previous message with the new one, the complete Hinglish conversion looks like this:

"Kyunki mujhe dar hai ki aap mujhse aage badh jaayenge. Jab maine start kiya kyunki mujhe guide karne waale koi nahi the toh main jhat se dhakke maar kar idhar se, udhar se..."

In English, this would roughly translate to:

"Because I'm afraid that you might get ahead of me. When I started because I had no one to guide me, I quickly pushed through from here and there..."

This translation is based on the continuation provided, though it appears the sentence may still be incomplete.
इंटरनेट से मुझे लिखना नहीं आता था। मेल कैसे मेल करते हैं, कैसे बात करते हैं?

The complete Hinglish version of the full statement is:

"Kyunki mujhe dar hai ki aap mujhse aage badh jaayenge. Jab maine start kiya kyunki mujhe guide karne waale koi nahi the toh main jhat se dhakke maar kar idhar se, udhar se, internet se mujhe likhna nahi aata tha. Mail kaise mail karte hain, kaise baat karte hain?"

Translated into English, it would read:

"Because I'm afraid that you might get ahead of me. When I started because I had no one to guide me, I quickly pushed through from here and there, struggling on the internet as I did not know how to write. How to send emails, how to communicate?"

This translation incorporates all the provided text, capturing the speaker's initial apprehension due to a lack of guidance and familiarity with digital communication tools.

इंटरनेट से मुझे लिखना नहीं आता था। मेल
कैसे मेल करते हैं, कैसे बात करते हैं?

The Hinglish version of the phrase "इंटरनेट से मुझे लिखना नहीं आता था। मेल कैसे मेल करते हैं, कैसे बात करते हैं?" is:

"Internet se mujhe likhna nahi aata tha. Mail kaise mail karte hain, kaise baat karte hain?"

This sentence translates to English as:

"I did not know how to write using the internet. How to send emails, how to communicate?"
कुछ नहीं, आधा नॉर्थ नॉर्मल तो मैंने वह

The Hinglish version of "कुछ नहीं, आधा नॉर्थ नॉर्मल तो मैंने वह" would be:

"Kuch nahi, aadha north normal toh maine woh"

It seems like the sentence is not complete, as it ends abruptly with "मैंने वह" (maine woh). The context isn't entirely clear without a complete sentence or additional information. However, the provided text converts directly to Hinglish as shown above.
